title: Leptorhabdos parviflora
taxonomy: Leptorhabdos parviflora is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with further classification within the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of a large number of flowering plant species commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Leptorhabdos is a small group of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. Leptorhabdos parviflora specifically belongs to the order Gentianales and class Magnoliopsida. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants within this family.
uses: Leptorhabdos parviflora is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. It has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. In addition, Leptorhabdos parviflora is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections.
